children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to limit the risks that the failure of a large depository institution (whether or not that institution is an insured depository institution) would pose to insured depository institutions.

children.heading: Aggregate limits on insured depository institutions’ exposure to other depository institutions
children.label: b Aggregate limits on insured depository institutions’ exposure to other depository institutions
children.text: The Board shall, by regulation or order, prescribe standards that have the effect of limiting the risks posed by an insured depository institution’s exposure to any other depository institution.

children.label: i
children.text: all deposits at the other depository institution;

children.label: ii
children.text: all purchases of securities or other assets from the other depository institution subject to an agreement to repurchase; and

children.label: iii
children.text: all guarantees, acceptances, or letters of credit (including endorsements or standby letters of credit) on behalf of the other depository institution;

children.label: B
children.text: all purchases of or investments in securities issued by the other depository institution;

children.label: C
children.text: all securities issued by the other depository institution accepted as collateral for an extension of credit to any person; and

children.label: D
children.text: all similar transactions that the Board by regulation determines to be exposure for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Exemptions
children.label: 2 Exemptions
children.text: The Board may, at its discretion, by regulation or order, exempt transactions from the definition of “exposure” if it finds the exemptions to be in the public interest and consistent with the purpose of this section.

children.heading: Attribution rule
children.label: 3 Attribution rule
children.text: For purposes of this section, any transaction by an insured depository institution with any person is a transaction with another depository institution to the extent that the proceeds of the transaction are used for the benefit of, or transferred to, that other depository institution.

children.heading: Insured depository institution
children.label: d Insured depository institution
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “insured depository institution” has the same meaning as in .section 1813 of this title

children.heading: Rulemaking authority; enforcement
children.label: e Rulemaking authority; enforcement
children.text: The Board may issue such regulations and orders, including definitions consistent with this section, as may be necessary to administer and carry out the purpose of this section. The appropriate Federal banking agency shall enforce compliance with those regulations under .section 1818 of this title
